model_input:
You are a DVOC (Draft-Verify-Action Loop) verifier. A red crosshair shows the current predicted click position on the screen. A fixed blue circle provides spatial reference.

Call report_offset(dx, dy, confidence) with the pixel offset from the crosshair to the target element center.
dx: pixels right (negative = left). dy: pixels down (negative = up).
confidence: 0.0 (uncertain) to 1.0 (certain).

Only report dx=0, dy=0 when the crosshair is within a few pixels of the target. If uncertain, set lower confidence so the loop keeps refining.

Task: Click the Profile link in the dropdown menu
Previous iterations:
  iter 0: at (638,124) said dx=12 dy=323 conf=0.80 -> (650,447)
  iter 1: at (650,447) said dx=0 dy=-112 conf=0.80 -> (650,335)
Call report_offset().

raw_message.reasoning_content:
The user wants me to click the "Profile" link in the dropdown menu.
The red crosshair is currently at (650, 335) according to the previous iteration, but the image shows it's actually lower, on "Settings".
Looking at the image:
- The crosshair is on the "Settings" option.
- The "Profile" option is above it.
- The distance between the "Profile" and "Settings" options looks to be about 40-50 pixels.
- The crosshair center is approximately at (650, 540) in screen coordinates if we look at the image, but wait, the previous iteration says (650, 335). Let me re-examine the image and coordinates.
- The image resolution seems to be 1000x500 or something similar. No, let's look at the crosshair relative to the "Profile" text.
- The red crosshair is currently on the word "Settings".
- The "Profile" link is one item above "Settings".
- The vertical distance from "Settings" to "Profile" looks like roughly 40 pixels.
- So I need to move up (negative dy).
- dx seems to be 0 as the crosshair is horizontally aligned with the center of the menu.

Let's re-evaluate the coordinates.
Current crosshair (red) is on "Settings".
Target is "Profile".
Vertical distance looks like about 40 pixels up.
So dy = -40.
dx = 0.
Confidence = 1.0.
